raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Is this review intended as a pre-submission check for a scientific venue (focusing on novelty and contribution), or as a clarity and correctness check for an internal audience (focusing on logical flow and writing polish)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The review should assess if the 'central contribution' is framed clearly and effectively.",
    "R1.2": "The review must verify the logical consistency of the problem formalization, from the two-level structure to the optimization objective.",
    "R1.4": "The review should provide a preliminary assessment of the claimed novelty of the evaluation framework against related work.",
    "R1.6": "The review is expected to improve the sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and flow of the technical description.",
    "RX.1": "The review must check if the assumptions underlying the formalization, such as the aggregate state representation, are clearly disclosed.",
    "RX.2": "The review must critically evaluate whether the language used, such as 'central contribution' and 'unified framework,' constitutes an overclaim.",
    "RX.3": "The review's feedback must remain strictly within the scope of improving this specific technical section.",
    "RX.4": "The review should consider if the problem formulation, particularly the quality-of-service penalty, overlooks potential negative impacts on vehicle owners.",
    "RX.5": "All feedback provided in the review must be directly traceable to specific statements or omissions in the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Is this review intended as a pre-submission check for a scientific venue (focusing on novelty and contribution), or as a clarity and correctness check for an internal audience (focusing on logical flow and writing polish)?

rationale.R1.1: The review should assess if the 'central contribution' is framed clearly and effectively.
rationale.R1.2: The review must verify the logical consistency of the problem formalization, from the two-level structure to the optimization objective.
rationale.R1.4: The review should provide a preliminary assessment of the claimed novelty of the evaluation framework against related work.
rationale.R1.6: The review is expected to improve the sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and flow of the technical description.
rationale.RX.1: The review must check if the assumptions underlying the formalization, such as the aggregate state representation, are clearly disclosed.
rationale.RX.2: The review must critically evaluate whether the language used, such as 'central contribution' and 'unified framework,' constitutes an overclaim.
rationale.RX.3: The review's feedback must remain strictly within the scope of improving this specific technical section.
rationale.RX.4: The review should consider if the problem formulation, particularly the quality-of-service penalty, overlooks potential negative impacts on vehicle owners.
rationale.RX.5: All feedback provided in the review must be directly traceable to specific statements or omissions in the provided text.
